reasoning: The document shows a fidelity composite score of 0.60, which sits at the bottom of the moderate quality band and requires human intervention. Both shingling recall (0.61) and precision (0.73) are significantly below acceptable thresholds, suggesting substantial content loss or text corruption that the word count ratio fails to capture. Given the high cost of false negatives, these metrics necessitate a human review despite the pipeline's heuristic routing.
action_items: Compare extracted text against the original PDF to identify missing sections indicated by the 0.61 recall.
Check for garbled text or OCR errors suggested by the low precision score.
Verify that the 1.0 word count ratio isn't masking 'word salad' or repetitive noise.
